Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Patriot,

On 11/21, the Democrat Attorney General in Arizona had the chance to do the right thing and DROP her wrongful prosecution against me – an _innocent_ 80-year-old man facing 20 years in prison.

---

When President Trump and I were arrested in the most violent jail in America, we proclaimed, “ _NEVER_ SURRENDER!”
---
Two years later, President Trump is back in the White House.
---
_**But I’m still fighting for my freedom!** And Democrats have RIGGED the case so that a federal pardon from President Trump is **_POWERLESS_** in _state_ court._

Let me put it bluntly: _you_ truly are my final hope.

name: JB Pritzker
subject: Something is happening in NH
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
It’s Governor JB Pritzker.
I’m emailing you today about New Hampshire’s Senate race because this is serious: **Senate Republicans’ hand-picked candidate, John Sununu, just jumped in to defeat Chris Pappas and flip this open Senate seat red.**
**[Before I go any further, I have to ask: Will you rush $5 or more to elect Chris Pappas and keep New Hampshire blue?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251126_1_pritzker.dP7ElG&refcode=em20251126-1&t=1&akid=3695%2E582294%2E3N1H8S)**
_Without Chris Pappas winning in New Hampshire, Democrats won’t have a chance at taking back a majority in the Senate._ That’s why this race is being targeted by GOP super PACs and Republicans across the country.
And most recently the NRSC — which pours millions into electing Republicans to the Senate — said they’re “all-in” on John Sununu.
This means New Hampshire will see historic levels of outside spending, and we have to be ready to fight back.
Chris is a proven fighter for working families — and he’s seen tough campaigns like this before. He knows it takes grassroots support to win a race like this. That’s why I’m reaching out to you today.
**[If you’re with me and ready to send Chris Pappas to the Senate, will you chip in $5 or any amount today to help him defend New Hampshire’s seat?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251126_1_pritzker.dP7ElG&refcode=em20251126-1&t=2&akid=3695%2E582294%2E3N1H8S)**

clean_body: It’s Governor JB Pritzker. I’m emailing you today about New Hampshire’s Senate race because this is serious: Senate Republicans’ hand-picked candidate, John Sununu, just jumped in to defeat Chris Pappas and flip this open Senate seat red. Without Chris Pappas winning in New Hampshire, Democrats won’t have a chance at taking back a majority in the Senate. That’s why this race is being targeted by GOP super PACs and Republicans across the country. And most recently the NRSC — which pours millions into electing Republicans to the Senate — said they’re “all-in” on John Sununu. This means New Hampshire will see historic levels of outside spending, and we have to be ready to fight back. name: Team Taylor
subject: Why this race matters so much
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251126-849&t=1&akid=849%2E294397%2ELSPecD)
With Governor Tony Evers announcing he won’t run for re-election, Wisconsin’s political future is more uncertain than it’s been in years.
Whoever is elected governor will wield enormous power over our state.
If that power is abused, the Wisconsin Supreme Court will be one of the only checks left.
That’s why this race matters so much.
The Wisconsin Supreme Court will decide whether our state remains fair, impartial, and committed to protecting the rights and freedoms of all Wisconsinites — or whether it becomes a rubber stamp for extreme special interests.
**Judge Chris Taylor has spent her career fighting for the people, protecting constitutional freedoms, and making sure everyone is heard and respected in our legal system.**
She’s ready to bring these values to our state’s highest court, but she can’t win this race without your support.
[**Will you contribute $10 today to help elect Judge Chris Taylor and protect a fair, independent Wisconsin Supreme Court?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251126_1_ak.RrXVcA&amount=10&refcode=em20251126-849&t=3&akid=849%2E294397%2ELSPecD)

Thanks so much,
Team Taylor

---
Peter,

Last week, Bernie Sanders—the self-proclaimed democratic socialist—threw his full weight behind my opponent in the United States Senate contest in which we’re candidates.

In his endorsement of Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, Sanders called her the fighter we need to “ _**stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests.**_ ”

**Translation:** She’s his pick to push for socialized, government-run healthcare, $17 minimum wage, higher taxes on job creators, and overturning Citizens United to flood D.C. with more big-government cash.

That’s not leadership.

**That’s the socialism I fought against overseas.**

I’ve trained allied navies in the South China Sea.

_**I’m Adam Schwarze—Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, capitalist warrior. I’ve deterred real enemies to protect free enterprise. Now I’ll deter D.C. weakness and socialist overreach.**_

As a _**Navy SEAL Officer**_ , I deployed _**nine times**_ across 70+ countries—from three combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman, to commanding a Crisis Alert Force in the Middle East, to humanitarian missions in Africa.

I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security.

That’s why I risked my life—to defend American capitalism, the engine that built the greatest nation on earth.

Now, I’m risking my career and professional reputation to run for the United States Senate in Minnesota. I’m the underdog, but Republicans have a very real shot at flipping this Senate seat for the first time in _**DECADES.**_

What I don’t have right now is money. _**[If you still believe capitalism is worth fighting for, would you please consider a small investment in my campaign today? $5 or even $10 will do if you can spare it.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=692d6edc407549be932373843849d9f7&_e=E43sS5zuejkfquDtlJzKXVkC4-bimhtPmrYsNHLusFr5Q3p_Wc-0Bs99-n7Uyqh2r5TlzkU2uqNAvRdZDtlg22V-6jmNWouF5bD2hArgri2NOEF2RC_BolmEANRr_bf9Qqka6Z9PQndzXGtsL8GnAA12fkXFrOyQqmBW2Qum3tV__alh3r_IBpT7-z1lfbKJqFlSULKBIVCbwt-yXk61VXt4T_UBKBryyOPaHulW2vvyDop6a6T6uHNO1GEhyrlHmO_wMBS8lnfrD3C9-iDQf8hx5e6hPQSYg-Vhwtcm2B-Xw6v1w9dvc6htn5THTz1l2Il1TuVuwniGW4E9W7sw4lKCgVZZhWqDsDytsFcXh-5zODeU5V34A-7t_R26xpaFY4g-ZrQppV2XdWK_f4SLyehuM_ez3PCI9IPE2ebnhXdhqmDjWo7CeIYIqS5WBnpBV_xdf0wKSNM1oHwTSekSzjbFslysdwpJ9FCOgIu90zvtu2QH3YOjoEjqf1yvODZPadALMOIIaj49DYGFq9KmidU3GdHqa36CIsnrEm3B5m-RoQB_bWjyO5lcFKatvYLCAQZYNqfAuoYVN8fl-Tsbg4V7GeJbhkN59l8XZBuyKTYXTKno7l-xjPETRQInzis4-SGwhyjK4IGDQfD89C-0mZ_uar4nKbrlIc748zVCtS3YUvlDVw8ffs0PEeAPU0Zugx140F8SpSt-JgCkcSkKexV-Rh1i45LlAH-0UcrBsS5ibPaZK31UBOsl62uF2EK7TgV11h-1vcGToi1mgtj_UA%3D%3D)**_

\- As Tim Walz’s right hand, she sued to defund ICE detention, letting illegals with records roam free—while fentanyl from cartel labs (_**fueled by open borders**_) kills 1,200 Minnesotans a year.

\- Now, with Bernie’s blessing, she’s eyeing the Senate to nationalize healthcare and tax away innovation.

President Trump is already restoring deterrence—tariffs on China, sealed borders, and energy independence.

He needs allies like me: a SEAL who’s deterred real enemies in war zones, not dreamed them up in committee rooms.

My opponent’s Bernie-backed vision? It’s the same failed playbook I’ve seen abroad—quietly obedient societies, where the human spirit withers.

_**he left’s poll shows a Republican within 2 points of flipping this seat. **_

We need _**FINANCIAL FIREPOWER**_ to counter Bernie’s political machine, which is equipped with hundreds of millions of dollars and an email fundraising list 10 miles long.

clean_body: I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security. | Peter, Last week, Bernie Sanders—the self-proclaimed democratic socialist—threw his full weight behind my opponent in the United States Senate contest in which we’re candidates. In his endorsement of Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, Sanders called her the fighter we need to “ stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests. ” Translation: She’s his pick to push for socialized, government-run healthcare, $17 minimum wage, higher taxes on job creators, and overturning Citizens United to flood D.C. with more big-government cash. That’s not leadership. That’s the socialism I fought against overseas. I’ve trained allied navies in the South China Sea. I’m Adam Schwarze—Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, capitalist warrior. I’ve deterred real enemies to protect free enterprise. Now I’ll deter D.C. weakness and socialist overreach. As a Navy SEAL Officer , I deployed nine times across 70+ countries—from three combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman, to commanding a Crisis Alert Force in the Middle East, to humanitarian missions in Africa. I’ve seen socialism crush economies firsthand: families starving under state control, initiative erased by endless mandates, freedom traded for false security. That’s why I risked my life—to defend American capitalism, the engine that built the greatest nation on earth. Now, I’m risking my career and professional reputation to run for the United States Senate in Minnesota. I’m the underdog, but Republicans have a very real shot at flipping this Senate seat for the first time in DECADES. What I don’t have right now is money. My socialist opponent’s record? \- As Tim Walz’s right hand, she sued to defund ICE detention, letting illegals with records roam free—while fentanyl from cartel labs (fueled by open borders) kills 1,200 Minnesotans a year. \- Now, with Bernie’s blessing, she’s eyeing the Senate to nationalize healthcare and tax away innovation. President Trump is already restoring deterrence—tariffs on China, sealed borders, and energy independence. He needs allies like me: a SEAL who’s deterred real enemies in war zones, not dreamed them up in committee rooms. My opponent’s Bernie-backed vision? It’s the same failed playbook I’ve seen abroad—quietly obedient societies, where the human spirit withers. he left’s poll shows a Republican within 2 points of flipping this seat. We need FINANCIAL FIREPOWER to counter Bernie’s political machine, which is equipped with hundreds of millions of dollars and an email fundraising list 10 miles long. Every dollar arms the next generation of American strength—and will help us bury Bernie’s agenda in Minnesota. For a better tomorrow, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan P.S: Deterrence works when your adversary believes you’ll act. China believes me. Bernie fears me. FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

name: NH GOP
subject: This year, we’re grateful for YOU
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern
body:
On the eve of Thanksgiving, we want to take a moment to share what we’re grateful for — and why your support has mattered so much.

| | | | | [|
---](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=23a58a10b4&e=f3aa6419cd)
| | | | | |

**Friend,** On the eve of Thanksgiving, we want to take a moment to share what we’re grateful for — and why your support has mattered so much.This year, Granite Staters stood together to protect what makes New Hampshire special. We successfully fought back against repeated Democrat efforts to impose an income tax and sales tax. We expanded Education Freedom Accounts so more families can choose the learning environment that works for their children. We strengthened bail reform to keep dangerous criminals off the streets. And we ensured New Hampshire remains a state that rejects sanctuary cities and puts public safety first.**These victories didn’t happen on their own. They happened because Granite Staters refused to let New Hampshire become another Massachusetts or New York. They happened because people like you stood with us.** And for that, we are thankful.**But as we gather with family and friends, one thing is clear — the fight isn’t over.** Next year, Democrats will pour millions into New Hampshire to undo everything we’ve accomplished. They want an income tax. They want to weaken public safety. They want to roll back education freedom. And they’re counting on conservatives taking this season for granted._We can’t let that happen._ If you want New Hampshire to remain the safest, most affordable, and most freedom-minded state in the country, we need to stay organized, prepared, and fully resourced heading into 2026.[**Before Thanksgiving begins, will you chip in HERE to keep the progress going?**](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=4b9a69e8c9&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [KEEP NEW HAMPSHIRE STRONG](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=0fa8a0758b&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [DEFEND OUR VICTORIES](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=c21c7b7fff&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [PROTECT NEW HAMPSHIRE’S FUTURE](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=6bbff2e299&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| |

From all of us at the New Hampshire Republican Party, we wish you and your family a safe, joyful, and blessed Thanksgiving.Thank you for standing with us, and for believing in what New Hampshire can be.With gratitude,NHGOP
---
| | | | | | | | | [View email in browser](https://mailchi.mp/nhgop/thanksgiving-gratitude?e=f3aa6419cd)
Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern. Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate Committee
New Hampshire Republican Party * 10 Water St * Concord, NH 03301-4844 * USA

name: Shontel Brown
subject: Thank you!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120022491/584993212/1806897946?refcode=EM_C_251125thanks_SB_90DO_4_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmYzYzg2Yy1kNGNhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IWk41Yln4CWwxJ3HWqvrLtv0siLtv4Kbk32AFajgVPs=) Hi Peter,

I wanted to reach out before Thanksgiving and express my gratitude for your ongoing support.

As Trump and his cronies in Congress continue to wreak havoc on the American people, it is an absolute honor to have your continued trust as I continue fighting to ensure healthcare premiums don’t skyrocket, that people don’t have to choose between putting food on their table or paying their rent or mortgage, and to defend our democracy from the unprecedented threats by this administration.

With you by my side, I will continue to work to ensure that we move our nation forward, not back, and defend our democracy for generations to come. We have a lot of work ahead of us, but I vow to work every day to deliver for Ohioans and people across the nation.

**Today, I wanted to say thank you for your support. I hope you are able to spend the next few days surrounded by your family and friends, enjoy good food, and relax. Thank you for staying in this fight with me.**

Together,

Shontel | Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. Will you join her and be the change we need?
---

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=180023645&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMDIzNjQ1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQxNjc2OTMsImV4cCI6MTc2Njc1OTY5MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.odl_8-BfMqtqCAl7w52fesx51VSbQjJhhCwUFxqxZ08)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvdGhlLXBzeWNob2xvZ2ljYWwtZWRnZS1vZi1ncmF0aXR1ZGU_.xsNequT3B1nixGbcZGw9YoyuQ2StS3Ew7nMyJBRybS0?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/the-psychological-edge-of-gratitude?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

This Thanksgiving, I'm grateful to have worn many hats in my life. I've been a paratrooper, congressman, Under Secretary of the Army, Wharton lecturer & West Point professor, and, for a brief but glorious summer, Kit-Kat maker at the Hershey factory. Every one of those jobs demanded something different from me. But the one constant that has always helped me succeed has been gratitude.
And nowhere did life teach me more about gratitude than my deployment in Iraq.

There are certain things no one tells you about war.

They tell you about the missions, the terrain, the human loss, the danger. What they don't tell you is that war will strip your life down to the smallest details you took for granted, such as simply going to the bathroom.

During the brutal stretch of August 2003, which we dubbed **["fire month](https://substack.com/redirect/d30b30ec-92c0-4a4c-9b11-6c1b05eff4b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**," we didn't really have plumbing. We eventually had Porta-johns, but in 130-degree heat, those were basically plastic ovens that stank to high heaven, each one swarmed with dozens of flies.
If my work resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..xcxM8OuzfM6lO3E-JyeQdTvmUqqQju1_8HLAEJcsueM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=180023645)
We showered maybe once a week. Mostly, we washed and shaved using water bottles and the side-view mirrors of our Humvee.
On top of the less-than-stellar conditions, Iraq was extremely dangerous.

Our brigade had 3,500 paratroopers; 19 of them didn't make it home. I personally knew many of them. Some I knew like brothers.
Iraq taught me how to embrace the suck and see moments of grace, big or small.

The way a bottle of cold water could taste like heaven.
The way a laugh could keep you sane.
The joy of hot showers.
Toilets without flies.
Hot meals.
A friend's comforting presence.

Gratitude kept us focused on what we still had instead of only what we feared losing next.
In isolation, these moments seem small, but over time they compound. Gratitude is a force multiplier behind strong minds, and once you've experienced it in the harshest conditions imaginable, you can't unsee its power everywhere else.

When I returned home from war, I began noticing small opportunities to apply the lessons of gratitude in everyday life. Now, two decades later in my work as a business executive, those same lessons continue to guide how I lead and how I show up for others.
##
**1.** **Gratitude Enhances Situational Awareness**
When leaders practice genuine gratitude, their attention shifts. They stop scanning only for threats and gaps, and start noticing strengths and opportunity for growth.
## **2.** **Gratitude Builds Team Resilience**
As human beings, we absorb each other's energy. If a leader is panicked, team performance suffers.

When a leader consistently expresses gratitude, particularly during difficult moments, they reframe adversity for the whole team.
"We're failing" becomes "We're adapting."
"We're behind" becomes "We're learning."
"We're overwhelmed" becomes "We can do this together."

This isn't magic. It's chemistry.
Gratitude literally changes brain function.
Gratitude boosts [dopamine and serotonin](https://substack.com/redirect/f03f4ef3-3eb8-4656-90a2-0339f2788203?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--both essential for problem-solving and emotional regulation and [reduces cortisol](https://substack.com/redirect/c5e7a6b8-fa36-42ca-90f0-a01cd73d788f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the stress hormone.
In combat, that could mean life or death.
In business, it means better engagement, creativity, and endurance.
## 3\. Gratitude Builds High-Trust Teams
People follow leaders who genuinely see them and make them feel like their work matters.

And here's a tip that needs reminding:
People don't just respond to your words. They respond to the energy you give off.

Gratitude pulls your body out of fight-or-flight.
A regulated leader creates a regulated team.
And a regulated team becomes a high-trust unit.

Think of trust as the compounding interest of leadership.
Every expression of gratitude is a deposit that builds trust.
Every missed opportunity or insecurity is a withdrawal.

People instinctively trust leaders with gratitude in their rhythm.
They feel safer. They communicate more openly. They perform more consistently.
When gratitude becomes part of the operating system, teams communicate better, take more initiative, and step into responsibility faster.
## **4\. Gratitude Improves Decision-Making**
Fear narrows the mind. Gratitude opens it.

Neuroscience shows that [gratitude quiets the amygdala](https://substack.com/redirect/79779005-053a-4ed9-987d-a8d0888f9d00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the part of the brain responsible for fear and overreaction and shifts activity into the prefrontal cortex, the part responsible for strategy, long-view thinking, and good judgment.
Leaders who operate from gratitude see a wider set of options. They collaborate more easily and create environments where people feel psychologically safe to speak up.
And better ideas follow, too.
***
The most resilient people I know, on battlefields and in boardrooms, are not the ones with the easiest paths. They are the ones whose challenges deepened their capacity to meet life with awareness and appreciation.
On November 20th, I had the privilege of interviewing former Secretary of State Tony Blinken on stage at the 2025 Turnaround Management Association (TMA) Awards. During our conversation, he shared a story that brought this truth into sharp focus.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/da2e530b-22c6-476e-ae54-d721c327dd65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Antony Blinken and I on stage during our conversation at the 2025 Turnaround Management Association Awards on November 20.
While we did talk about policy and American politics, the most compelling thing we discussed was gratitude - raw, human gratitude and how it shaped his family.
He told the story of his stepfather, Samuel Pisar, who, as a young boy, survived four years in concentration camps during the Holocaust. Near the end of the war, he was forced on a brutal death march. At one point, he and a few friends made a desperate decision to escape. They broke away, fled into the Bavarian forest, and hid for days -starving, freezing, and unsure if they would live to see another sunrise.
One day, they heard a tank in the distance. They had seconds to choose: stay hidden and risk dying, or run toward the unknown.
They ran.
As they emerged through the trees, they saw the five-pointed white star painted on the steel of an American tank. A U.S. soldier opened the hatch, looked at this emaciated boy, and Blinken's stepfather said the only English words he knew:
"God bless America."

Who would have guessed that 70 years later, he'd have a step-son who would become National Security Advisor to the Vice President--and later the 71st Secretary of State--and sit in the White House with President Obama and our national security leaders during the daring operation that finally brought Osama bin Laden to justice.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/abb8cdca-25d4-4664-a70d-6302560ca441?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
President Barack Obama, Vice President Joe Biden, Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, and senior national security leaders watch the unfolding raid on Osama bin Laden in the White House Situation Room on May 1, 2011. Antony Blinken--National Security Advisor to Vice President Biden--can be seen standing in the back, fourth from the right. Pete Souza / The White House
***
Practicing gratitude does not erase hardship, but it does create an affirmation of connection and purpose.

These are the people others naturally turn to. Not because they are flawless but because they are grounded and rooted in what matters.

So look for the good. Say it out loud as affirmation. Give it away generously - especially this Thanksgiving.

And let the people in your life who matter know it.

Happy Thanksgiving! I'm grateful for you!
-- Patrick

body:
I will never take your sacrifice for granted.
[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=9a63a30b898a4398a1e7ef91294bcb08&_e=3EOZqvJCGj5m4QvWrGgfGARHcbhv80NF0kd2u_vurlPPOi76CMusHvRm-RdrGpKAQMFD1XV2IeehC17QTEZA6X_RihLLoWXPXLKXsXN0fE2nAXKVso9uN8F_YjefSuMTNxNRn39eV1lI0ILMwFPmgaJkFxCRH-fLE64i1Q8balJ8CyZwQTChqFQ1Doy4OxfbTy-Vn9yigN1rQU31nibs2BxIGldRYksNdff098Mxgg_kXWr6K1iDi4x_PBtUY7pZ4cyCVmvrVFzP-SDILsBp9k1nVjFCODU-MEvAuuW1ReOyYRkBZdFx2FIs2bGUDFFKMmNujNKhbKGGfR6FM1YE2d_s4ryUllo6WZ42GUo4kS3QmEYdZV4CnKrFjbKu8EQoXj2vnWENxoWQg9HYvLRhpXCfa_umMjXctHUWCMRCuaDD-O7yONw-exPsainsHF1ND4oxG0egOdO1ZBVW01fGOf6A_wIBW1jip3WrxSLJaw4HZimW2sABLD3ca6oCjI3tHYH_2f69GVo6WSEn07bERgPvE0WE7j3oQ1bmHaII8rry1ood1BemMbAVb5tWUpKyKmMDBKt7p08Dq0AzyCZ6kCOOrs6U2H_O6d8eOlV44YJ7FWvbBdH1UCjoc4p7AsC2Y1gOUdL-2FCe0IdyYwKQ-liHtQ0uqPvoHBr6LzuZ-iWAHwuFjCd2t0VEijIwhd7KkESfkXE1vbg4FnCcPu9W0Smqo9tv2HsEMqnTvZgY5kb_9XR7sRtaXVYu2yza9Y3KOE7wc69mUtF8Km58eXX-sBDDmviz8zJARdSVB3d-QiNWscX_w1yarrPzp9u6krd4tOsjRziymLGKGsD9_fjaXr7BBOKPneFKsciemev2PxoQ1PqXvPm9ihS4HwRK5tpn90-M6eZQTf_BHK2gx9Mut1gEyz9vPEmgjJQTiDsK7sk%3D)Politics today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks.
But I want to cut through the noise for a moment.

clean_body: I will never take your sacrifice for granted. today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That 's why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. And if you haven 't given yet - - I'd be honored to have you on my team. Every single gift, no matter the size, fuels this must-win fight. grateful,Vivek Ramaswamy the election were held today, we 'd come up just short.We can't afford to lose this ground -- not now, not after everything we've built.Your support right now makes the difference between a comeback and a collapse.**| | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201

name: Chuck Rocha
subject: Meet JoCasta Zamarripa 12/3 – Running for WI Secretary of State
body:
Meet the candidate who can reshape Wisconsin’s future in 2026. | | | |
---
| | | Good morning,

Before we head into Thanksgiving, I wanted to share a quick reminder about our upcoming [in-person fundraiser](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=451af5f0f1&e=b8f90417f9) for [JoCasta Zamarripa](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=8556a86068&e=b8f90417f9), candidate for Wisconsin Secretary of State. With one of the most competitive open seats in a Trump-carried state, this race remains a critical opportunity to secure a Democratic trifecta in 2026 and strengthen voting rights in Wisconsin.

If you haven’t had a chance to RSVP yet, please register through [ActBlue](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=c38210ad79&e=b8f90417f9) or RSVP with [Saenz@SolidarityStrategies.com](mailto:Saenz@SolidarityStrategies.com?subject=RSVP%20for%20JoCasta%20Zamarripa%20Fundraiser&body=Please%20rsvp%20me%20for%20this%20event%20and%20send%20me%20more%20details.%20Thansk!).
**Fundraiser for JoCasta Zamarripa for Wisconsin Secretary of State
Wednesday, December 3, 2025
5-7 P.M. ET
Washington, DC** [_**Click here**_](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=b6ea6030b6&e=b8f90417f9)[ _****__**to RSVP**_](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=0c98e7d36d&e=b8f90417f9)
**(Location Provided Upon RSVP)**
---
|
---
| [ ](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=2d49ff6f99&e=b8f90417f9)
---
|
---
| **About JoCasta and her race:**
Born and raised on Milwaukee’s south side, JoCasta has spent her career fighting for working families and expanding opportunity, making history as the first Latina and first openly bi member of both the Wisconsin State Legislature and Milwaukee Common Council. With early backing from Rep. Mark Pocan and 18 Democratic state legislators, her decade in the Assembly and leadership as Alderwoman for Milwaukee’s 8th District show she’s ready to deliver for the entire state. Her candidacy can energize Wisconsin’s fast-growing but underrepresented Latino electorate, boost Democratic participation, and bring new urgency to a Secretary of State office long overlooked after 40 years under the same Democrat. This office is essential for securing fair maps, protecting elections, and stopping MAGA threats to democracy.
---
|
---
| Consider supporting JoCasta’s historic campaign **[ONLINE](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=b261f9d4f8&e=b8f90417f9) **or reply to this email to confirm your attendance if supporting by mail:
**JoCasta for WI
5027 W. North Ave
Milwaukee, WI 53208 **

Your support can make all the difference in lifting a historic candidate and energizing Wisconsin’s growing Latino electorate.

Wishing you and your loved ones a warm and restful Thanksgiving. We look forward to seeing you next week.

Thank you,
Chuck Rocha

name: Wes Moore
subject: What I heard in the past few weeks
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)
Peter, in the past few weeks I’ve had the chance to speak to many Marylanders who support our mission to leave no one behind.
**What almost everybody said was that we have to stand up for Maryland, no matter what the Trump administration throws our way.**
That’s who Marylanders always have been. We stick up for each other. Marylanders are tough, Marylanders are resilient, and Marylanders do not need performative antics -- we need results.
**That’s the kind of leadership we deserve, and it’s why I’m running for reelection. To win, I’ll need your help.**
**[If you’re with me, will you chip in any amount so we can reach our November fundraising goals before the deadline? Here’s a link so I know it’s coming from you:](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251126-1.dR522a&refcode=em-20251126-1&t=2&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)**
**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20251126-1?refcode=em-20251126-1-dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251126-1.dR522a&refcode=em-20251126-1&t=3&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)**
Thank you, Peter!
Elevate,
Wes
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251126-1.dR522a&refcode=em-20251126-1&t=4&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20251126-1&t=5&refcode2=980_1086043_03_cZv&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=980%2E1086043%2E03_cZv)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Haley Stevens
subject: 5 reasons to chip in $5
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
help us close the gap

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9245?t=2&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
---
Hi null, it’s Haley with a quick morning update.

Yes, yesterday’s emergency finance meeting was tough. **But after I reached out, this grassroots team stepped up in a huge way.** And I’m so, so grateful.
But we still need to raise another $32,700 before midnight tonight. **[So here are five quick reasons to chip in $5 right now:](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9288?t=3&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)**
**1\. We have to stop the chaos.** Between the longest shutdown in U.S. history, cuts to SNAP, and surging health care costs – working families are ready for strong, stable leadership.
**2\. Trump’s tariffs are hitting Michigan hard.** They’re hurting the manufacturing industry I’ve spent my career fighting to protect. And we need more public servants speaking up.
**3.****The GOP megadonors are circling.** Outside spending groups and billionaires are zeroing in on Michigan, trying to buy this seat for Republican Mike Rogers.
**4.****We can win.** Polls show I’m the strongest Democrat running – I’m consistently leading in the primary and polling the best against Mike Rogers in the general.
**5.****The affordability crisis is crushing hardworking Americans.** It's no secret that folks are struggling.**** I'm running for Senate to do what I’ve always done: fight for working families. But I can’t do it alone.
Null, this goal is critical. If we fall short, we risk falling behind just as national Republicans ramp up their attacks.
**[Will you rush $5 before midnight to help us close the gap and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9288?t=4&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)**
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9247?t=5&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9248?t=6&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9249?t=7&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9246?t=8&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
---
With grit,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9287?t=9&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9251?t=10&akid=1470%2E49239%2E9hvs1q)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Americans for Responsible Innovation
subject: The Fight Against AI Law Preemption
body:
ARI organizes letters, releases analysis, and launches new opposition tracker

| | | | | |
---
|
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=c79ad8967b&e=a07d75591f)
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
Over the past week, a handful of policymakers launched another attempt to slip AI law preemption into must-pass legislation in Congress. The latest effort to freeze state AI laws comes late in the NDAA process, despite the fact that House- and Senate-passed versions of the NDAA did not include AI preemption. No text of the scope or length of the proposed AI preemption effort has been publicly released.**Here’s the good news** : leaders across the country are raising their voice to oppose the latest attempt at an AI law moratorium.
Over the past week, we’ve seen state lawmakers and faith leaders send letters to Congress, we released new polling on where the American public stands, and we launched a new tool to track the growing opposition.
---
| |
### STATE LAWMAKERS SPEAK OUT
This Monday, over [290 state legislators](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=87f8e0f3aa&e=a07d75591f) across 43 states urged Congress to reject AI law preemption in the NDAA. The bipartisan group, which ARI helped to organize, warned that a blanket prohibition would "abruptly cut off active democratic debate in statehouses" and risk nullifying laws states have already passed to protect consumers, patients, workers, and creators. As the letter notes, states serve as laboratories of democracy, and after years without comprehensive federal action on privacy and social media harms, Congress shouldn't freeze state progress.
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=6a151fd87a&e=a07d75591f)
| |
### VOTERS CONTINUE TO OPPOSE PREEMPTION
[New polling](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=9c9376d097&e=a07d75591f) continues to show strong, bipartisan opposition to AI preemption.A national YouGov/Institute for Family Studies poll found Americans oppose adding preemption to the NDAA by a 3-to-1 margin (57% oppose, 19% support) – with majorities across party lines, income levels, and age groups.
In collaboration with Morning Consult, ARI released state-level polling this week in [Michigan](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=12b0fa6369&e=a07d75591f), [Georgia](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=291219cdb1&e=a07d75591f), and [Colorado](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=057fc87e1f&e=a07d75591f). The findings are consistent: majorities in all three states say states should have the ability to pass AI laws, and voters are far more likely to support candidates who oppose preemption than those who favor it.
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=423a25078f&e=a07d75591f)
| |
### FAITH LEADERS JOIN THE FIGHT
In a [separate letter to congressional leadership](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=506945d4fe&e=a07d75591f), faith leaders from across the religious and political spectrum called preemption a threat to communities of faith and conscience. Signers included the National Association of Evangelicals, the National Latino Evangelical Coalition, Mormon Women for Ethical Government, the Center for Public Justice, and dozens of faith leaders and religious scholars. The letter frames AI governance as a moral question: "What are the impacts, benefits, and dangers to the dignity of work, human intellect, the nature of truth, the boundaries of consent, and the social compact we share?
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=692b693af2&e=a07d75591f)
| |
### NEW TOOL: TRACKING OPPOSITION VOICES
ARI launched a [new interactive tracker at NoAILawBan.org](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=0602551f75&e=a07d75591f), cataloging the lawmakers, governors, and organizations across the political spectrum speaking out against AI law preemption.
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=1c611ee328&e=a07d75591f)
| | The fight against AI preemption is growing. Whether it's coalition letters, new analysis, or tools to track the opposition, we're building the case for responsible AI governance, one step at a time.Thanks for being part of the ARI community.

---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | [](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=df65b54c9d&e=a07d75591f)
---
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=6f2ba1b165&e=a07d75591f)
---
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=b066e32130&e=a07d75591f)
---

---

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: A last-ditch request from me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Normally when I write to you via email, I like them to be different from the other messages you get from most elected officials.
Peter —
Normally when you see my name in your inbox, I like the messages to be different from the other emails you get from most elected officials.
I've written to you in detail about the depth of corruption and self-enrichment of the Trump Administration, the President's thirst for political retribution and intimidation, along with other things I am seeing or working on in Washington, D.C. and at home in Georgia.
This message is different. It's more direct.
I am reaching out today with a last-ditch request for a contribution to my campaign before our end-of-month fundraising drive comes to a close. Here's why:
This race will be one of the most competitive and expensive in the nation. I am the only Democratic Senator up for re-election in a state Trump won in 2024 and that makes me MAGA’s #1 target.
In 2020, the pundits said flipping a Republican Senate seat in Georgia couldn’t be done. But we built one of the strongest voter mobilization programs in Senate campaign history — and we won. That victory won the Senate majority.
Now we must win again. And we will win again.
Just like last time, I can’t do it alone.
So I’m asking:
**[Please split a contribution between my re-election campaign and Heartland Patriots today before our end-of-month fundraising drive comes to a close.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98272?refcode=em20251126-9763&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9763%2E614464%2EvdNEhy)**

name: Congressman Al Green, TX-09
subject: Why they are spending Millions to beat us
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/111773/images/AL%20Green/green%20take%20it%20back.jpg]
Friends,
If you see a slick negative ad about me in your feed today, check the fine print.
It’s not coming from a grassroots group in Houston. It’s coming from AIPAC and the dark-money machine.
They are pouring cash into our district for one reason: Punishment.
They want to punish me for standing up for the human rights of innocent Palestinian men, women, and children. Just like the President wants to punish me for standing up for the Constitution.
They think they can buy this seat. They think that if they spend enough millions, they can silence me.
Are we going to let them?
I am fighting the Supreme Court to protect your vote. I am fighting the President to protect your democracy. And now, I am fighting the special interest lobby to protect your voice.
I am proud of the enemies I have made. Now I need my friends.
Chip in $10 or whatever you can. Let’s show them that our movement is not for sale.
In the fight
Congressman Al Green
FIGHT BACK [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/251101em]
Keep Al Green in Congress Campaign

Keep Al Green in Congress Campaign
PO Box 56761
Houston, TX 77256

GOP Rep. Lisa Mclain fired back at Trump-derranged Wolf Blitzer for comparing President Donald Trump's military orders to those of the Nazi's murdering jews in WW2.
**[Her response was perfect, watch it right here:](https://substack.com/redirect/a0539cc9-06ca-4c0a-a5dc-843eed09133d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[](https://substack.com/redirect/a0539cc9-06ca-4c0a-a5dc-843eed09133d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[ Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/a0539cc9-06ca-4c0a-a5dc-843eed09133d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **Mother of White House press secretary 's nephew detained by ICE**
Bruna Caroline Ferreira, described by DHS as **["a criminal illegal alien from Brazil"](https://substack.com/redirect/7e89bce0-62d2-42a8-bf73-f9ed117d5861?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** who shares an 11-year-old son with Karoline Leavitt's older brother Michael, was taken into custody in Revere, Massachusetts after allegedly overstaying her tourist visa that expired in June 1999 and is being held at the South Louisiana ICE Processing Center awaiting removal proceedings.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7e89bce0-62d2-42a8-bf73-f9ed117d5861?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Leavitt has no comment, source claims child lives with father:** Karoline Leavitt, 28, told WMUR she had no comment on Ferreira's arrest, while a source said the women have not spoken in many years and claimed "the child has lived full-time in New Hampshire with his father since he was born. He has never resided with his mother," with Michael Leavitt stating his only concern is "the safety, well-being, and privacy of my son."
* **Lawyer contests arrest, claims she maintained DACA status:** Ferreira's attorney Todd Pomerleau said she "maintained her legal status" under the Obama-era DACA program and "is in the process of actually getting her green card," insisting "Bruna has no criminal record whatsoever" and calling her "a great mom" who was "abruptly arrested and taken from her young child right before Thanksgiving," though DHS claims she was once arrested on suspicion of battery.
* **Arrest part of Trump 's mass deportation campaign:** The detention comes as Trump and his team have organized a "mass deportation campaign" involving ICE, Border Patrol, and National Guard operations across the U.S., with DHS stating "all individuals unlawfully present in the United States are subject to deportation," while a GoFundMe set up by Ferreira's relative says she was brought to the U.S. as a child in 1998 and "has done everything in her power to build a stable, honest life here."

* * *
# **Universities host "decolonizing" Thanksgiving events**
The University of California, Davis hosted a Zoom event called **["Decolonizing Thanksgiving in the Classroom"](https://substack.com/redirect/eeca80d6-3551-47c3-9f39-ea8b4354e0ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to discuss "reframing classroom practices" by "centering perspectives from Turtle Island," while MIT students were invited to a "4th annual Thanksgiving Myth-busting" event including a "Buffy the Vampire Slayer" viewing and a trip to the National Indigenous Day of Mourning rally in Plymouth, Massachusetts.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/eeca80d6-3551-47c3-9f39-ea8b4354e0ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Schools refer to Thanksgiving as "Day of Mourning":** The University of Massachusetts called Thursday a "Day of Mourning" on its DEI website, while Albuquerque Public Schools sent students a November update explaining that "many native people do not celebrate" Thanksgiving because it is a "reminder of the genocide of millions of Native people, the appropriation of Native lands, and the erasure of Indigenous cultures," with Berkeley Unified School District also using the term "mourning" in its teaching guide.
* **Education watchdog defends traditional holiday:** Paul Runko of Defending Education told Fox News Digital that "Thanksgiving is meant to bring people together, not to divide students or cast blame over heritage," noting that "236 years ago, President George Washington urged Americans to set aside a day to give thanks for the blessings and abundance of our nation" with presidents throughout history continuing that tradition.

* * *
# **Tennessee special election competitive in Trump-Won district**
A Tennessee congressional race is defying expectations, with Republican Matt Van Epps **[holding only a narrow advantage](https://substack.com/redirect/e5e59ff7-dc60-4d36-921b-0939ece9ad97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** over Democratic challenger Aftyn Behn, who expresses how she openly hates Nashville, TN culture, in a district Donald Trump carried by 22 points in 2024, according to fresh polling data.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d979c6a4-e20a-47d8-901f-003887d9d277?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Razor-thin margin:** Van Epps leads Behn 48%-46% among likely voters in Tennessee's 7th Congressional District, per an Emerson College Polling/The Hill survey, with turnout patterns favoring the Republican among Election Day voters and the Democrat among early voters.
* **Demographic battleground:** The race reveals sharp divisions, with voters under 40 backing Behn at 64% while those over 70 support Van Epps at 61%, alongside a gender gap showing men favoring Van Epps by nine points and women preferring Behn by six points
* **National implications:** A Democratic victory on December 2 would slash the House GOP majority to just three seats, following Republican losses in recent New Jersey and Virginia elections, while Trump's approval sits narrowly underwater at 47%-49% in the heavily conservative district

The contest fills the vacancy left by former Rep. Mark Green, who departed Congress earlier this year for the private sector.
* * *
# **Trump delays Ukraine peace summit, despite Zelensky 's Thanksgiving timeline**
President Trump abruptly postponed hopes for an imminent **[peace agreement](https://substack.com/redirect/9a49f067-8bc6-4170-b318-6e9e4f1e86a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to end the Russia-Ukraine conflict, announcing he would dispatch envoys to both nations rather than meet directly with leaders until a final deal emerges.
* **Summit hopes crushed mid-interview:** Ukrainian presidential advisor Andriy Yermak learned in real-time during a Post interview that Trump posted on Truth Social he was sending Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to Ukraine and Special Envoy Steve Witkoff to Russia, abandoning his earlier Thanksgiving deadline for ending the war.
* **19-point plan in limbo:** Yermak had anticipated Trump and Zelensky would sign a peace proposal at Mar-a-Lago over the holiday weekend, but the agreement--negotiated Sunday between U.S. and Ukrainian officials--requires resolution of sensitive issues and faces the major hurdle of securing Russian acceptance.
* **Urgency from Kyiv:** Ukrainian officials stressed the devastating human cost of delays, with Yermak emphasizing that daily Russian missile attacks claim civilian lives and asserting only American pressure through sanctions and global influence can force Moscow into negotiations.

Trump indicated he would only meet with Zelensky and Putin once a peace deal reaches its final stages, despite Ukraine's push for immediate action.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

But you can finally get the full truth by . P.S. They want to keep buried... so there’s no telling what they’ll do to stop . Watch it now while it’s still available. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. What Makes Parler Different

The recent Cloudflare outage disrupted banks, streaming platforms, payment systems, and major consumer apps. AWS has suffered recent similar outages. Overnight, the internet was reminded of one uncomfortable truth: when much of the world’s digital infrastructure is powered by a few companies, everyone can seem to be vulnerable. Parler learned this lesson years ago when AWS removed us without warning. Instead of rebuilding on rented infrastructure, we decided to build our own.

Parler’s Advantage: Total Independence

Parler now operates a fully independent technology ecosystem spanning cloud, CDN, blockchain, social, video, commerce, and payments. The company works to minimize dependencies and eliminate single points of failure. Outside providers do not have the ability to silence, throttle, or disrupt the network. This independence is not just philosophical. It helps create real world stability for creators, merchants, and media companies. It is what management believes makes Parler distinct and unique.

The 506(c) Offering

Parler is raising capital under Regulation D 506(c). Accredited investors can participate as the company continues preparing for a planned IPO. No guarantees are being made. The company is sharing its current expectations. Parler’s owned tech stack gives creators more independence and potential growth. Efficiency and opportunity come from infrastructure control, not Big Tech reliance.

How You Can Support the Mission

If you are not accredited, you can still help expand the movement toward independent infrastructure. Share

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
