Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

# [Inflation: Time to Talk Turkey](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=179962383&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5OTYyMzgzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQxNzEwOTAsImV4cCI6MTc2Njc2MzA5MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.HKFo0CQCCp-1akCVJCxjjFDKoywxzNJuQmQd_aeXEFc)
### What the cost of Thanksgiving dinner tells us about the economy...
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)

---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d903b17c-128c-4c8c-a5b1-42f5b7f088e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Thanksgiving is a time of traditions: driving or flying to visit family and friends, watching the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade, strategizing for Black Friday sales, and of course, enjoying a turkey dinner. Another tradition, rumored to have begun with President Truman, is [the annual pardoning of turkeys by the US President](https://substack.com/redirect/f26349ba-be50-4c31-be4d-714272a426da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And this year was no different when President [Trump pardoned Gobble and Waddle](https://substack.com/redirect/df31def3-2681-4e65-9960-94baf1bc746d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[](https://substack.com/redirect/26922589-928b-44cc-8bf0-0d7ddc827a46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
More importantly, Trump made reference to the price of turkeys, noting that they had fallen dramatically. Inflation has been a major political and economic issue over the past few years, but it now seems like every single holiday is seized by political opponents to describe how bad inflation really is (or isn't). What's the cost of hot dogs and hamburgers this Independence Day versus last year? How does a bucket of Halloween candy compare to last year?
Thanksgiving is no different, and given the increasing weight of affordability (food, housing, etc) in the minds of voters, it's not surprising that the price of a turkey dinner is a topic both incumbents and challengers want to use to spin a narrative. Surely there is an objective answer to the question of "Did the cost of a Thanksgiving dinner increase or decrease this year?" And that answer presumably sheds light on the state of inflation and the health of the economy, right? **Sadly, no.** Depending upon who you ask, you can get wildly different answers.
#### The "Cheaper" Camp
The [American Farm Bureau Federation](https://substack.com/redirect/76dc1813-abe2-496e-bf0e-37f87758823a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) says that the average Thanksgiving dinner for 10 people will cost $55.18, down 5 percent. [Wells Fargo](https://substack.com/redirect/319c4ca9-9bca-4581-9aee-9b9a95202e51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) estimates that the cost has decreased by 2-3 percent. And every year Walmart announces the cost of its Thanksgiving meal, using a mix of brand names and its private label offerings. This year its offering can feed a family of 10 for [less than $4 per person](https://substack.com/redirect/65d5e65a-ca50-4c1c-a492-e7fc1fbd40cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), its lowest cost yet. While this is down 25 percent, Walmart doesn't necessarily use the same products in its basket every year.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7b34ce82-73f8-4976-b922-646acc2be3eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: Walmart.com
#### The "More Expensive" Camp
Meanwhile, the [Deloitte Thanksgiving Index](https://substack.com/redirect/4d2f33c6-3726-480d-bba3-03d7dc6dc8f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reports that the cost to feed a family of eight is up a modest 0.6 percent. [The Associated Press (AP)](https://substack.com/redirect/721daf62-b129-47b0-bf63-066747e16155?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) says it's higher, reporting that a basket of 11 Thanksgiving staples is $58.51, 4.1 percent more expensive this year than last year, and the progressive thinktank [The Century Foundation](https://substack.com/redirect/fe572147-7886-4546-9d17-d61fe54fb26d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) calculates that Thanksgiving costs jumped a whopping 9.8 percent.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fbde150d-12eb-45a0-a9f5-25abadedf44b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The Deloitte Thanksgiving Index [https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/consumer-products/cost-of-thanksgiving-dinner-meal.html](https://substack.com/redirect/4d2f33c6-3726-480d-bba3-03d7dc6dc8f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Just to be clear, the perspectives I shared (and I'm sure there are more!) mean the price of a Thanksgiving meal has either fallen by as much as 25% per person or risen by 10%? Why the wide range? _Well, simply put, a meal is not a meal is not a meal_. The price of a Thanksgiving dinner (unsurprisingly) depends on what you have as part of your dinner. The mix of products has a big impact on the total price, and further, annual changes to the meal's composition make year over year comparisons impossible. Imagine if one estimate included lobster mac and cheese with mini-cubes of filet mignon drizzled with truffle oil for this year, when the prior year had baked potatoes?
While the cost of some items (like stuffing and rolls) appears to have risen this year, the cost of others (like mashed potatoes and pumpkin pie) went down. So, the cost of Thanksgiving dinner is going to vary depending on what the meal includes as well as where the ingredients or meal was purchased.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..R7MB3vtjpQjVDOBdPaWZiPFNi0xH9bVVOP_gsW_DDNo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=179962383)
But there is one near universal constant when it comes to Thanksgiving: the turkey. And because it usually accounts for a disproportionate share of the meal's total cost, perhaps we could simplify our analysis by just focusing on the price of the bird? For the most part, turkey prices are down. Datasembly sees a 2 percent decrease in the price of 10 pound turkey while Deloitte has it at a modest 0.4 percent increase. This year, Walmart's Butterball Turkey is $0.97 per pound, its lowest price since 2019.
So great, inflation is down! _Not so quick._ Might lower turkey prices be an active retail strategy in a time of falling consumer confidence? Some grocers use turkeys as loss-leaders to get shoppers in the door, knowing that they'll also buy side dishes and desserts (presumably at higher prices with greater profits to the retailer). Maybe turkey prices don't tell the story, even if they do help consumers at the checkout register.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/227032b3-dbd7-4a74-b7e8-a9a86c808557?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Jonathan Cooper](https://substack.com/redirect/ed51dc14-9f35-4f15-8ef3-fcbe3d3daf83?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/1fe74a14-d094-48c4-9d34-46770372dbe2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_So, what does all of this mean about the state of the economy?_ _Not much_. Unless there is consistency in the specific items purchased across years, measurements are nothing more than fodder for new articles and political debates. Sadly, it does seem the stories you read about Thanksgiving inflation are presented with a political bent. Data can be (and often is) manipulated to support almost any narrative.
**So here's my best advice for navigating Thanksgiving inflation uncertainty** : Instead of stressing about the implications of turkey or cranberry prices for your 401(k) or future car payments, enjoy an extra helping of stuffing and grab an extra piece of pie. Don't worry about what it means. After all, we'll soon be debating the price of Christmas trees.

clean_body: What the cost of Thanksgiving dinner tells us about the economy... | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? What the cost of Thanksgiving dinner tells us about the economy... | | | Nov 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Thanksgiving is a time of traditions: driving or flying to visit family and friends, watching the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade, strategizing for Black Friday sales, and of course, enjoying a turkey dinner. Another tradition, rumored to have begun with President Truman, is . And this year was no different when President . More importantly, Trump made reference to the price of turkeys, noting that they had fallen dramatically. Inflation has been a major political and economic issue over the past few years, but it now seems like every single holiday is seized by political opponents to describe how bad inflation really is (or isn't). What's the cost of hot dogs and hamburgers this Independence Day versus last year? How does a bucket of Halloween candy compare to last year? Thanksgiving is no different, and given the increasing weight of affordability (food, housing, etc) in the minds of voters, it's not surprising that the price of a turkey dinner is a topic both incumbents and challengers want to use to spin a narrative. Surely there is an objective answer to the question of "Did the cost of a Thanksgiving dinner increase or decrease this year?" And that answer presumably sheds light on the state of inflation and the health of the economy, right? Sadly, no. Depending upon who you ask, you can get wildly different answers. The "Cheaper" Camp The says that the average Thanksgiving dinner for 10 people will cost $55.18, down 5 percent. estimates that the cost has decreased by 2-3 percent. And every year Walmart announces the cost of its Thanksgiving meal, using a mix of brand names and its private label offerings. This year its offering can feed a family of 10 for , its lowest cost yet. While this is down 25 percent, Walmart doesn't necessarily use the same products in its basket every year. | | ---|---|--- Source: Walmart.com The "More Expensive" Camp Meanwhile, the reports that the cost to feed a family of eight is up a modest 0.6 percent. says it's higher, reporting that a basket of 11 Thanksgiving staples is $58.51, 4.1 percent more expensive this year than last year, and the progressive thinktank calculates that Thanksgiving costs jumped a whopping 9.8 percent. | | ---|---|--- The Deloitte Thanksgiving Index Just to be clear, the perspectives I shared (and I'm sure there are more!) mean the price of a Thanksgiving meal has either fallen by as much as 25% per person or risen by 10%? Why the wide range? Well, simply put, a meal is not a meal is not a meal. The price of a Thanksgiving dinner (unsurprisingly) depends on what you have as part of your dinner. The mix of products has a big impact on the total price, and further, annual changes to the meal's composition make year over year comparisons impossible. Imagine if one estimate included lobster mac and cheese with mini-cubes of filet mignon drizzled with truffle oil for this year, when the prior year had baked potatoes? While the cost of some items (like stuffing and rolls) appears to have risen this year, the cost of others (like mashed potatoes and pumpkin pie) went down. So, the cost of Thanksgiving dinner is going to vary depending on what the meal includes as well as where the ingredients or meal was purchased. But there is one near universal constant when it comes to Thanksgiving: the turkey. And because it usually accounts for a disproportionate share of the meal's total cost, perhaps we could simplify our analysis by just focusing on the price of the bird? For the most part, turkey prices are down. Datasembly sees a 2 percent decrease in the price of 10 pound turkey while Deloitte has it at a modest 0.4 percent increase. This year, Walmart's Butterball Turkey is $0.97 per pound, its lowest price since 2019. So great, inflation is down! Not so quick. Might lower turkey prices be an active retail strategy in a time of falling consumer confidence? Some grocers use turkeys as loss-leaders to get shoppers in the door, knowing that they'll also buy side dishes and desserts (presumably at higher prices with greater profits to the retailer). Maybe turkey prices don't tell the story, even if they do help consumers at the checkout register. | | ---|---|--- Photo by on So, what does all of this mean about the state of the economy? Not much. Unless there is consistency in the specific items purchased across years, measurements are nothing more than fodder for new articles and political debates. Sadly, it does seem the stories you read about Thanksgiving inflation are presented with a political bent. Data can be (and often is) manipulated to support almost any narrative. So here's my best advice for navigating Thanksgiving inflation uncertainty : Instead of stressing about the implications of turkey or cranberry prices for your 401(k) or future car payments, enjoy an extra helping of stuffing and grab an extra piece of pie. Don't worry about what it means. After all, we'll soon be debating the price of Christmas trees. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. name: Jeff Jackson
subject: Politics during Thanksgiving
body:
And our SNAP win, CBP, and a big A.I. settlement

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9qZWZmamFja3Nvbm5jLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.D6rqJyqROw2s_OpWcnXB2F8NHzIqXNhLL7-e8XG82VI?) for more
---
# [Politics during Thanksgiving](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=180028160&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMDI4MTYwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQxNzIyNDcsImV4cCI6MTc2Njc2NDI0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KTz-PW_eqGZkF4FDY_JCbLpyy3AMT3ATLL0l6CcEENM)
### And our SNAP win, CBP, and a big A.I. settlement
| | [Jeff Jackson](https://substack.com/@jeffjacksonnc)
---
| Nov 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jeffjacksonnc)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=180028160&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMDI4MTYwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NDE3MjI0NywiZXhwIjoxNzY2NzY0MjQ3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTE0NTAyMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZVZRGYYlVdQUpO1ec4yhNHagJ6FMVBof6qX95crEdrQ&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=180028160&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMDI4MTYwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQxNzIyNDcsImV4cCI6MTc2Njc2NDI0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KTz-PW_eqGZkF4FDY_JCbLpyy3AMT3ATLL0l6CcEENM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=180028160&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMDI4MTYwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQxNzIyNDcsImV4cCI6MTc2Njc2NDI0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KTz-PW_eqGZkF4FDY_JCbLpyy3AMT3ATLL0l6CcEENM)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvamVmZmphY2tzb25uYy9wL3BvbGl0aWNzLWR1cmluZy10aGFua3NnaXZpbmc_._UG668qNRp7u2iJ4nk1t0JhbZNmYk2nmoeN3CFYIW7A?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/jeffjacksonnc/p/politics-during-thanksgiving?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Last time we spoke, I told you we were going to court to protect SNAP.
Feels like a long time ago, but it was just a few weeks.
It was a huge fight.
To recap:
We got a letter from the Agriculture Department saying that they were cutting off SNAP on November 1 because of the shutdown. They had an emergency fund but said they wouldn't use it.
We took them to court and a judge immediately agreed that they had to use their emergency fund. Why? Because that's why Congress gave them the fund - _for an emergency._ The court held that the department didn't have the discretion to simply refuse.
But the fund didn't have enough to cover a full month, so the court ordered the Ag Department to pay out 50% of all benefits. That was the situation when the shutdown ended and full payments resumed.
Here's the bottom line: Because of our combined legal effort, there was a critical window where 1.4 million people in our state had access to SNAP who otherwise wouldn't have. That includes 600,000 kids and 40,000 babies.
Our team at NCDOJ worked around the clock, and it made a huge difference for a lot of families. I'm really proud of them.
## **Immigration scams**
We all saw the federal CBP operation last week. What you may not have seen are the attempts to scam Hispanic families.
When enforcement tactics are overly broad - as they were here, even detaining American citizens and lawful residents - it creates confusion and vulnerability. Scammers know this, and they're using it to scam Hispanic families out of thousands of dollars by pretending to be immigration attorneys or government officials.
These scams are highly sophisticated and come in multiple forms.
Sometimes they'll use the names and photos of real immigration attorneys, message Hispanic families directly on social media, demand a large up-front payment to help them - and then disappear.
Or they'll pose as a fake law firm and stage a video call designed to look like a court hearing. Someone will pretend to be a judge and will tell the family to pay thousands of dollars to their fake attorney. In some cases, A.I. has been used to make it look legitimate.
We're pushing out warnings as fast as we can - and we will use every state law available to pursue the scammers.
As for the federal operation, it was plainly designed to generate fear - and it did. Instead of a targeted effort focused on dangerous offenders, it became a broad sweep of people who fit a demographic profile. When federal law enforcement operates in North Carolina, they should meet the standard of discipline and professionalism we expect from our local officers. I work with law enforcement every day as AG, and I know firsthand you can respect people's rights and still go after serious criminals.
Over the last two weeks, I've spent a lot of time working with local elected officials, community leaders, and nonprofits. Even under deliberate provocation, our state responded with calm, coordination, and compassion. Much of the work wasn't public, but it mattered enormously in the moment.
I also appreciate Sen. Tillis calling on Homeland Security to provide a list of everyone who was detained and whether they've been released. That information is crucial and I appreciate him recognizing that. Given that this was a federal operation, his federal role is uniquely positioned to ensure accountability, and we need him to follow through on that work.
## **A.I. rent-hiking case**
One of the first things I did as AG was sue six of the biggest landlords in the state for using artificial intelligence software called RealPage to illegally collude and raise rents.
The largest one - Greystar - just settled with us. It's a $7 million settlement, and they can no longer use that rent-setting method.
They control 25,000 rental units in North Carolina. That means those families are now going to be charged a fairer price at a time when housing costs are already hitting people hard.
This is the second major landlord to settle. We have four more to go - plus RealPage itself.
## "What about politics at Thanksgiving dinner?"
Over the next few days, many of you will be with family celebrating the holiday. I sometimes get asked how to handle the political conversations that surface at family dinners. My general advice is to remember that the people around that table are part of your life, not part of your feed. Not every disagreement needs to be settled in real time, and stepping away from a debate doesn't diminish your convictions - it just means that relationships matter, too.
These days, I think it's easy to over-consume political content. As much as I hope people are well-informed, at a certain point you're just acting compulsively and locking yourself into perpetual anxiety. That's not healthy for you or helpful to anyone else.
So, for the next few days, give yourself the grace to focus on friends and family. Political fights can wait. Exhale. Go outside. Notice who's laughing in the kitchen. Those small moments aren't a distraction from the world - they're the reason it's worth working to improve.
Just my advice. Obviously, it's up to you.
But I sincerely hope this holiday gives you a chance to breathe, reconnect, and spend time with people who make you feel grounded. Wishing you a peaceful and meaningful Thanksgiving.
Best,
Jeff Jackson

name: Team Bianco for Governor
subject: Lara Trump’s discussion with Sheriff Bianco
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BIANCO FOR GOVERNOR 2026 FPPC # 1479095
body:
They said California was lost to the radical Left, but Lara Trump just confirmed what we already know: the voters are actually running in the opposite direction. Watch the interview that proves the nation is finally taking notice of our movement. | Peter, America has noticed that something is happening in California. Lara Trump just sat down with Sheriff Bianco to discuss exactly what we are seeing on the ground: **While Democrat leaders try to drag California further left, the voters are running in the opposite direction.** The nation is noticing what we’ve known for weeks: Sheriff Bianco is leading this race. name: Ilhan Omar
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
The night we won our first race for Congress.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1968%2E791427%2Eq_h91G)
**August 14th, 2018.**
That was the night we won our first primary race for Congress.
Just a few years before that moment, nobody would have guessed that a Muslim, refugee, woman of color could win a seat on the national stage.
But we shattered all of those expectations, and shattered some glass ceilings, too.
And we did it through the sheer power of progressive community organizing and a commitment to a bold, forward-thinking agenda for Minneapolis and the entire nation.
**August 11th, 2026.**
That’s the day our campaign will be tested once again, and voters will head to the polls to decide if they will reelect me and our movement.
Eight years after that fateful night in 2018, I want to prove that our campaign is not only ready to win once again, but that our movement has grown bigger, stronger, and even more committed to defeating the far-right and passing our progressive agenda.
So, August 11th may be a good few months away, but we have to start **preparing right now** for the tough fight ahead.
We could be up against multiple challengers, with outside spenders like AIPAC swooping in to try to buy this race with millions of dollars in attack ads. We can’t be caught off-guard when the stakes are this high.
That’s why I'm asking: **[Chip in to my campaign today and help us be ready for anything that comes our way in this race. We can make history again and continue the long fight to transform America, but only if everyone does their part.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/8?c0=io-em-nov25.r_en0_&refcode=em251126-1-180&t=2&akid=1968%2E791427%2Eq_h91G)

name: Mark Kelly
subject: My take on the Senate special elections… –
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hi, DSCC Vice Chair Mark Kelly here. I want to talk to you about a _key_ opportunity in front of us as we fight for the Senate majority: **special elections.**

I know a thing or two about these kinds of races, because I got to the Senate by _winning_ a special election back in 2020 — even as Republicans spent over $70 million to stop me. **And my victory proved mission-critical, because without it, Democrats wouldn't have had a Senate majority.**

[**Now, history could repeat itself. Two special elections coming up in Ohio and Florida are among a small handful of races that could decide the majority — again. So I'm coming to ask you, will you donate $5 to win races like those and take back the Senate? >>**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21835%2E9716520%2ErsnU9V)

| | [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21835%2E9716520%2ErsnU9V)
---

I want to be clear, team: **These special elections give us an opportunity we would have waited YEARS for without them, and they make a BIG difference.**

In fact, during Donald Trump's first term, Democrats flipped **_three_** Senate seats in special elections, including mine, my good friend Raphael Warnock's, and even a seat in dark-red Alabama. Each time, we defeated Trump-endorsed candidates who would have been guaranteed votes for his destructive agenda, and instead we elected Democrats who fought for hardworking Americans.

**Now, we're facing the same task again — but it won't be easy.** Trump is reportedly "obsessed" with the midterms, National Republicans will spend big-time to keep their majority, and just like when I first won, Democrats only need to flip a handful of seats to take back the Senate.

**Take it from someone who's been in this position before: We'll need your help to make the most of this opportunity — and that's why I'm reaching out.**

**[As a DSCC Vice Chair, I'm personally asking: Will you chip in $5 or whatever you can to help Democrats win in states like Ohio and Florida and take back the Senate? We have a real opportunity to topple Trump's majority, and we can't let it slip away >>](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=9&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21835%2E9716520%2ErsnU9V)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

| [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=10&amount=5&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251126-MarkKelly-KellySpecialElectionsFR-Pros-sl1&akid=21835%2E9716520%2ErsnU9V)

clean_body: | | | Hi, DSCC Vice Chair Mark Kelly here. I want to talk to you about a key opportunity in front of us as we fight for the Senate majority: special elections. I know a thing or two about these kinds of races, because I got to the Senate by winning a special election back in 2020 — even as Republicans spent over $70 million to stop me. And my victory proved mission-critical, because without it, Democrats wouldn't have had a Senate majority. | | I want to be clear, team: These special elections give us an opportunity we would have waited YEARS for without them, and they make a BIG difference. In fact, during Donald Trump's first term, Democrats flipped three Senate seats in special elections, including mine, my good friend Raphael Warnock's, and even a seat in dark-red Alabama. Each time, we defeated Trump-endorsed candidates who would have been guaranteed votes for his destructive agenda, and instead we elected Democrats who fought for hardworking Americans. Now, we're facing the same task again — but it won't be easy. Trump is reportedly "obsessed" with the midterms, National Republicans will spend big-time to keep their majority, and just like when I first won, Democrats only need to flip a handful of seats to take back the Senate. Take it from someone who's been in this position before: We'll need your help to make the most of this opportunity — and that's why I'm reaching out. We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately. | | | | Thank you for your support. Mark Kelly Vice Chair, DSCC Former U.S. Navy combat pilot, engineer, and NASA astronaut Mark Kelly is a senator from Arizona and a Vice Chair of the DSCC. He's fighting to lower costs for Arizonans, hold Trump accountable, and create good-paying jobs. | Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and an astronaut. name: Jon Ossoff for U.S. Senate
subject: $1 to stand with Jon before our end-of-month fundraising deadline hits in just a few days
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Good morning, Peter! We’re reaching out to ask you to pitch in **$1** to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign so we can go into Thanksgiving in a strong position to hit our November goals. **Yes, just $1.**
We understand that $1 might not sound like much, especially when Republicans have already spent more than $10 million — and pledged millions more — trying to flip Jon’s seat red...
But when thousands of grassroots supporters are all chipping in $1 at a time, it adds up fast.
And, Peter, _we know this strategy works._ In 2021, this team was able to defy the odds and win a historic runoff election alongside Rev. Warnock and secure the Senate majority — all because of $1, $10, and $25 donations from supporters just like you.
Now, Jon is running in what is likely to be the most expensive and competitive race in the nation. And he’s counting on us once again.
**So with time running out to hit our goals ahead of Thanksgiving and our end-of-month deadline, we are respectfully asking:**
[**Can you please chip in $1 — or more if it feels right — to Jon’s reelection today? Chip in now and we’ll put it right to work building a campaign that will win an OVERWHELMING victory on Election Day.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251126-1-f-b&t=1&akid=1159%2E5691347%2EXFFEc0)

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
#

Folks, as you read this, I was on Jimmy Kimmel’s show last night.
This has been quite a week for our country. I’m grateful Jimmy gave me the opportunity to speak about what’s been happening over the past several days.
If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. Because now, the FBI has asked to question me and other members of Congress.
All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear.
If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it.
I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.
If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming.
And if you’d be willing to help support me as we gear up to fight back against the attacks coming our way from Donald Trump and his Administration, please:
[**Make a donation. It would mean a lot to me.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-nov25-dod/.t5EZ4X&refcode=em251126-1-700k&t=1&akid=6264%2E6688874%2ESXFpPz)

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
|
#
Friend, I’m wishing you and your loved ones a very happy Thanksgiving! Every year, our family loves to share what they’re most thankful for. It’s a simple tradition, but it never fails to remind me how important it is to pause, reflect, and appreciate the people who make life truly meaningful. As we head into the holiday season, I’ve been thinking a lot about how grateful I am – for my family, for this community, and for supporters like you who believe in the work we’re doing. Serving MI-08 is one of the greatest honors of my life. The conversations I’ve had with families across our district ground me every single day. And they remind me exactly what we’re fighting for. Thank you for being part of that fight. Thank you for believing in our shared future. And thank you for all the ways, big and small, that you make our community stronger. I hope this time brings you good food, good company, and a Lions win tomorrow! 💙 -Kristen

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: President Trump is using the FBI to target me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
Derek —
The FBI's Counterterrorism Division appeared to open an inquiry into me in response to a video the President did not like.
In that video, I directly quoted the Uniform Code of Military Justice alongside five other Members of Congress with service and veteran backgrounds. Officers and enlisted in our military are trained to follow lawful orders, and refuse to follow illegal orders. It’s part of the training. This has been the case since World War II.
Since then, the President has misquoted me, and then called for my arrest, trial, and death by hanging. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has threatened my friend, Senator Mark Kelly, with court martial. And now, it appears the president has directed the FBI to target the rest of us.
**This is exactly why we made that video in the first place.**
President Trump believes in weaponizing the federal government against his perceived enemies and does not believe laws apply to him or his Cabinet. He uses legal harassment as an intimidation tactic, to scare people out of speaking up. He’s done it to law firms, universities, private sector companies and private individuals.
But these kinds of tactics won’t work on me. And neither will the total wave of death threats, bomb threats, and general harassment he has unleashed.
I have always worked to be upfront and honest in these emails. This latest news from the FBI means that I have had to start thinking through a legal defense strategy – and we do not know exactly where President Trump and his Department of Justice will take this inquiry. And for the time being, all of that planning is being paid for through campaign dollars.
**[So, your split contribution to Adam Schiff and me will be put to work immediately to ensure I have the resources to fight back.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2907?c0=abs_em_tan_slotkin.inwVhp&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20251126_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=3&akid=9238%2E5257348%2EpvsMkU)**

To be frank, the whole thing isn’t about one line from one video. The events of the last week do not represent the country we know and love.
I refuse to believe that this is the new normal — where the President of the United States uses fear and intimidation against people with whom he disagrees.
But Adam Schiff and I can’t do this alone.
Thank you for reading — and thank you in advance for your help.
Elissa Slotkin

clean_body: | Derek — The FBI's Counterterrorism Division appeared to open an inquiry into me in response to a video the President did not like. In that video, I directly quoted the Uniform Code of Military Justice alongside five other Members of Congress with service and veteran backgrounds. Officers and enlisted in our military are trained to follow lawful orders, and refuse to follow illegal orders. It’s part of the training. This has been the case since World War II. Since then, the President has misquoted me, and then called for my arrest, trial, and death by hanging. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has threatened my friend, Senator Mark Kelly, with court martial. And now, it appears the president has directed the FBI to target the rest of us. This is exactly why we made that video in the first place. President Trump believes in weaponizing the federal government against his perceived enemies and does not believe laws apply to him or his Cabinet. He uses legal harassment as an intimidation tactic, to scare people out of speaking up. He’s done it to law firms, universities, private sector companies and private individuals. But these kinds of tactics won’t work on me. And neither will the total wave of death threats, bomb threats, and general harassment he has unleashed. I have always worked to be upfront and honest in these emails. This latest news from the FBI means that I have had to start thinking through a legal defense strategy – and we do not know exactly where President Trump and his Department of Justice will take this inquiry. And for the time being, all of that planning is being paid for through campaign dollars. Patriot,

I learned firsthand how our culture despises achievement and wishes to destroy anyone willing to challenge the status quo, big government, or the Fake News media machine.

When I was ousted from Project Veritas, two big things (one you haven't heard about) happened to me.

FIRST: The media launched a vicious smear campaign against me like I've never seen before. **Make no mistake:** _They identified this as an opportunity to "cancel" me forever_ and tried everything in their power to destroy me and everything I've ever stood for.

They failed.

SECOND: I received an OVERWHELMING amount of support from patriots across the country. These brave men and women defended me, but more importantly, they stood up for the principles of liberty and free speech.

**One of my top supporters was The Atlas Society.**

Jennifer Grossman, the CEO of The Atlas Society, proudly stood by my side and even invited me to speak with a gathering of the organization's top student leaders about free speech and how Howard Roark, the fictional hero of _The Fountainhead_ by Ayn Rand, remains my inspiration to act with integrity.

Which is why I am personally reaching out to you.

I told Jennifer I would help The Atlas Society raise $25,000 in the next 48 hours by personally contacting my online supporters. [Please consider making an URGENT donation of any amount to The Atlas Society by clicking here.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=311a395edf574e5abb14cef474360f34&_e=_Loq5exEadOUrdD5_uD109QlRNDYhEnBMOMXzyRY5cdYGoutwqeuzKjzaiiu5IyW8uUZTb0fF-c0v3JxBnjvnLKgJGwPW6YlVM-w0XJY71hWzroPcb23V26szsEFPgV0jRswYACvV_Nl6xeHIFal7fnLtzBhTkMLBt3wkMOj7jFGLEHDGngGUkAfyuZUoMOiiWPwYQVQr1pocHvO0D4yWnUm_J8HoA0_FIB9ytFA8m3g5KpKMjZqy9JEuR_Y1KIBtszv1_hziJnPtKKpg-VAPq6foAQN-eDDoHVViu5ozwNFf0KmVUKLmby4OS30JMyqiDlUDJAqmHcyguVIYB2-PUhfj9UAFASmFtmRoHFUnsVvHyF_BLUKIv0-NSeJ_y_rAHyCIThMcTVFM2SEr7ilXBv6Yj0c0L3sP2TstPEaBmIhb9I1nP5m80JR09XVCtJbf04B-SgfNm0UGgfD_tzT08aL8syA1nzEA_2o0htaIxrVoi6iMgfgbJWmXlFiiPL-apqAAhLglDNucGCTjtT1LAWUP7AzSFy461Zps2RTDnDHlAdN45BVCjLaWbdkDXK_q0P8zRqvLp3WYAsJZFJ0eUyiABQFeM_JeyBIBSKkLDkAOUQcpkqrhlGc32JNARyaVzH1TjvE5kp5RvE4FhNLfg%3D%3D)

Let me tell you more about The Atlas Society and how they are fundamentally changing the direction of our country.

_I will also tell you HOW The Atlas Society is spreading its message in what I consider one of the most innovative methods I 've EVER seen a nonprofit organization engage in before._

_**_So keep reading_!**_

My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, reminds us that politics is downstream from culture. But what is culture downstream from? Answer: Philosophy. That's where The Atlas Society shines, engaging young people with the virtues of reason, achievement, and benevolent self-interest in wildly creative, artistic ways.

Have you ever read Ayn Rand's _Atlas Shrugged_ or _The Fountainhead_?

These books changed how I saw the world and my role in it.

But they are 700+ pages long!

**Jennifer shared this terrifying statistic with me:** Daily reading of books among young people has fallen from 70% in the late 1970s to 12% today.

So, how do we preserve Rand's role as the greatest all-time recruiter for liberty? That's where The Atlas Society gets creative! They leverage art and technology to create programming for "_skimmers, swimmers, and deep divers_."

---
**Skimmers:** Social media memes and animated videos -- every single one of their 50 posts a week gets 40K comments, shares, and likes. The "Draw My Life" videos on concepts like Capitalism, Property Rights, and Free Speech reach MILLIONS of viewers. Links invite young people on a journey for deeper intellectual exploration.

**Swimmers:** Graphic novel adaptations of Ayn Rand's shorter books, Pocket Guides that distill big concepts like Postmodernism and Socialism into a 90-minute read. Weekly podcasts with top entrepreneurs and thought-leaders, and discussion groups like "Morals & Markets."

**Deep Divers:** They recruited an all-star academic faculty of philosophers and economists who guide students on a deeper study of Rand's philosophy, publish independent work, and provide programming at their summer conference for students (the Galt's Gulch Summit, where I spoke!).
---

Jennifer and The Atlas Society are accomplishing great things for our country, and I hope you will be one of the FIRST supporters of mine to make a donation and help me reach our $25,000 goal. Please use one of these links to donate:

clean_body: Help me empower young people The Atlas Society proudly stood by my side. | | Patriot, I learned firsthand how our culture despises achievement and wishes to destroy anyone willing to challenge the status quo, big government, or the Fake News media machine. When I was ousted from Project Veritas, two big things (one you haven't heard about) happened to me. FIRST: The media launched a vicious smear campaign against me like I've never seen before. Make no mistake: They identified this as an opportunity to "cancel" me forever and tried everything in their power to destroy me and everything I've ever stood for. They failed. SECOND: I received an OVERWHELMING amount of support from patriots across the country. These brave men and women defended me, but more importantly, they stood up for the principles of liberty and free speech. One of my top supporters was The Atlas Society. Jennifer Grossman, the CEO of The Atlas Society, proudly stood by my side and even invited me to speak with a gathering of the organization's top student leaders about free speech and how Howard Roark, the fictional hero of The Fountainhead by Ayn Rand, remains my inspiration to act with integrity. Which is why I am personally reaching out to you. I told Jennifer I would help The Atlas Society raise $25,000 in the next 48 hours by personally contacting my online supporters. Let me tell you more about The Atlas Society and how they are fundamentally changing the direction of our country. I will also tell you HOW The Atlas Society is spreading its message in what I consider one of the most innovative methods I 've EVER seen a nonprofit organization engage in before. So keep reading! My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, reminds us that politics is downstream from culture. But what is culture downstream from? Answer: Philosophy. That's where The Atlas Society shines, engaging young people with the virtues of reason, achievement, and benevolent self-interest in wildly creative, artistic ways. Have you ever read Ayn Rand's Atlas Shrugged or The Fountainhead? These books changed how I saw the world and my role in it. But they are 700+ pages long! Jennifer shared this terrifying statistic with me: Daily reading of books among young people has fallen from 70% in the late 1970s to 12% today. So, how do we preserve Rand's role as the greatest all-time recruiter for liberty? That's where The Atlas Society gets creative! They leverage art and technology to create programming for "skimmers, swimmers, and deep divers." Skimmers: Social media memes and animated videos -- every single one of their 50 posts a week gets 40K comments, shares, and likes. The "Draw My Life" videos on concepts like Capitalism, Property Rights, and Free Speech reach MILLIONS of viewers. Links invite young people on a journey for deeper intellectual exploration. Swimmers: Graphic novel adaptations of Ayn Rand's shorter books, Pocket Guides that distill big concepts like Postmodernism and Socialism into a 90-minute read. Weekly podcasts with top entrepreneurs and thought-leaders, and discussion groups like "Morals & Markets." Deep Divers: They recruited an all-star academic faculty of philosophers and economists who guide students on a deeper study of Rand's philosophy, publish independent work, and provide programming at their summer conference for students (the Galt's Gulch Summit, where I spoke!). Jennifer and The Atlas Society are accomplishing great things for our country, and I hope you will be one of the FIRST supporters of mine to make a donation and help me reach our $25,000 goal. Please use one of these links to donate: Ultimately, here is what The Atlas Society is fighting FOR: Free market capitalism Rational self-interest Respect for achievement and success Free speech The future of young people and our great country And here is what The Atlas Society is fighting AGAINST: Cancel Culture Our Educational system indoctrinating young people to embrace socialism Our sick culture that demonizes success and embraces twisted ideas * Big government I am inviting you to support The Atlas Society with a donation of ANY amount. As a personal favor to me, please make your donation within the next 48 hours so I can help Jennifer reach our goal of $25,000. Thank you for everything, James O'Keefe P.S. Here is a picture of me speaking with The Atlas Society's top student leaders. These students inspire me. Sadly, young people are learning to embrace socialism, reject critical thinking skills, and shed their creativity. Well, NOT these students. The Atlas Society is teaching students that they CAN accomplish great things, become leaders, and inspire others to do the same. Thank you, James The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: This is top secret
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=em20251126-1358&t=1001&akid=1375%2E251025%2EXlBWYB ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=em20251126-1358&t=1002&akid=1375%2E251025%2EXlBWYB ]Abdul for US Senate

Hi Peter,

With Thanksgiving right around the corner, I wanted to share something
special with you.

Between campaign stops and calls, I called my grandparents, who sifted
through an old family folder and found my great-grandmother’s recipe for
scalloped oysters, a dish my family has made for Thanksgiving for as long
as I can remember.

It’s rich, indulgent, and absolutely not cardiologist-approved. But it
tastes like home.

Technically, this is a top-secret family recipe, passed down from one
generation to the next via a handwritten index card:

[ A ]A preview of the family recipe, handwritten on an index card.

However, I’m willing to make an exception and send it to you, under one
condition: [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251126-1-ak.KaKyl1&amount=10&refcode=em20251126-1358&t=1004&akid=1375%2E251025%2EXlBWYB ]Donate any amount to fuel our campaign, and I’ll give you my
family’s famous scalloped oysters recipe.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251126-1-ak.KaKyl1&amount=10&refcode=em20251126-1358&t=1005&akid=1375%2E251025%2EXlBWYB ]Donate ››

Even if you just chip in $1, that’s meaningful. Every bit of support
allows us to run this campaign without corporate influence.

It’s that spirit of showing up for each other, the way families do, that
keeps us focused on building a government that truly fights for working
people.

Last call,
Peter. [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251126-1-ak.KaKyl1&amount=10&refcode=em20251126-1358&t=1006&akid=1375%2E251025%2EXlBWYB ]Give any amount to
keep our campaign firing on all cylinders, and I’ll share my family’s
scalloped oysters recipe as a thank you.

I hope your Thanksgiving is full of warmth and gratitude. This movement
gives me all of that and more. ♥️

Thank you,

Abdul

name: Hector Mujica
subject: Our Florida waters are in danger
body:
Trump is throwing away decades of precedent and destroying our waters.
#
So much of why I love Florida (besides the people of course!) is tied to just how beautiful our state is.
People from across the world know Florida for our beautiful beaches and crystal blue water. Trump is trying to take that away. **He plans to destroy our ocean and accelerate climate change and destruction by opening our oceans for oil drilling.**
Trump’s newest proposal would open Florida’s coastal waters to federal oil drilling auctions. These waters haven’t been open to new drilling _for decades._
We need leaders in Congress who will fight to protect our water, our land, and our earth. I refuse to take even a dime of fossil fuel money. **[Can you add your name today to join me in the fight to protect the delicate ecosystem of our state?](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22083?source=em20251126-1371&t=1&akid=1371%2E2118727%2EuIPkO9)**
**[Add your name](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22083?source=em20251126-1371&t=2&akid=1371%2E2118727%2EuIPkO9)**
This is just the latest in Trump’s tirade to roll back any and all sustainability progress we’ve made. Climate change only increases costs for everyday working people. The price of energy rises, and it’s us who will be first in line when natural disasters hit Florida.
If Trump can get this proposal off the ground, Florida will be paying the price for decades. This isn’t the type of leadership we need. We need honest and accountable leaders who will fight for what’s best for ALL Floridians.
[**My promise to you is that I won’t back down to Trump. I’m a fighter, and I’m ready to go to bat for you, for our planet, and for our state. Help me get there by joining this fight today.**](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22083?source=em20251126-1371&t=3&akid=1371%2E2118727%2EuIPkO9)
Thank you,
Hector

**LeftNet is an online political network fighting for progressive causes and candidates focused on social, racial, economic, and environmental justice.**

name: Mark Kelly
subject: President Trump is calling for me to be hanged -
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
**President Trump is calling for me to be hanged.**

I put out a video, alongside veterans of the military and former members of the intelligence community. It had a simple message: If you are serving in the military, you have an obligation to refuse illegal orders.

Trump’s friends in the White House didn’t like that. They accused us of insurrection.

At some point, the President heard about it, and he hasn’t stopped posting and reposting about us since — accusing us of sedition, calling for our arrest, calling for us to be hanged.

It is no small thing for the President of the United States to call for your execution. Elon Musk called me a traitor. Trump’s advisor, Roger Stone, called for me to be executed. And now Trump himself has called for me to be hanged.

As someone whose family has faced political violence before, I know you have to take words like this seriously, and that’s why I’m writing to you.

There’s no telling what kind of storm we’re heading into right now. Unfortunately, we need to be prepared for any outcome.

clean_body: President Trump is calling for me to be hanged. I put out a video, alongside veterans of the military and former members of the intelligence community. It had a simple message: If you are serving in the military, you have an obligation to refuse illegal orders. Trump’s friends in the White House didn’t like that. They accused us of insurrection. At some point, the President heard about it, and he hasn’t stopped posting and reposting about us since — accusing us of sedition, calling for our arrest, calling for us to be hanged. It is no small thing for the President of the United States to call for your execution. Elon Musk called me a traitor. Trump’s advisor, Roger Stone, called for me to be executed. And now Trump himself has called for me to be hanged. name: Ximena, United We Dream
subject: Immigrant-led, community-powered
body:
Donations from supporters like you fuel United We Dream’s efforts to empower and protect our loved ones. | | | Peter — we’re led by immigrant youth and powered by community. That means that this team’s contributions do **so much** for our people-powered movement. **As part of our End-of-Year campaign, we wanted to show you just a preview of your impact:**
* Thanks to this team’s support, United We Dream was able to organize calls with immigrant youth after the _Texas Dream Act_ was reversed, along with their eligibility for in-state tuition.

* Donations that we receive help us send emails with important and timely information during rapid response moments, like when eligibility for the Affordable Care Act was stripped from DACA recipients.

* Your support helps make it possible for us to host trainings and summits, which offer a safe space for immigrant youth and allies to learn, connect, and build collective power for lasting change.

* And so much more!

In the face of increasing attacks on our communities this year, you've helped us respond, keep our neighbors informed about their rights, and empower immigrant youth and allies. **Now, we’re reaching out to humbly ask:** **** ******** **[Will you make your first contribution to United We Dream, Peter? clean_body: Donations from supporters like you fuel United We Dream’s efforts to empower and protect our loved ones. | | | Peter — we’re led by immigrant youth and powered by community. That means that this team’s contributions do so much for our people-powered movement. As part of our End-of-Year campaign, we wanted to show you just a preview of your impact: Thanks to this team’s support, United We Dream was able to organize calls with immigrant youth after the Texas Dream Act was reversed, along with their eligibility for in-state tuition. Donations that we receive help us send emails with important and timely information during rapid response moments, like when eligibility for the Affordable Care Act was stripped from DACA recipients. Your support helps make it possible for us to host trainings and summits, which offer a safe space for immigrant youth and allies to learn, connect, and build collective power for lasting change. And so much more! In the face of increasing attacks on our communities this year, you've helped us respond, keep our neighbors informed about their rights, and empower immigrant youth and allies. Now, we’re reaching out to humbly ask: ** **** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | The respective merchants of record for transactions to United We Dream Network are ActBlue Charities Inc. They receive and process those donations and subsequently give them to the organization or cause indicated on the contribution form within 30 days. This donation is tax-deductible. ** Donations from supporters like you fuel United We Dream’s efforts to empower and protect our loved ones. This work only grows more vital by the day. Thank you for standing with us. — Ximena, United We Dream | Follow us on: | | | | |

name: Aftyn Behn
subject: Donald Trump’s panicking about this race
body:
Peter, this is Aftyn Behn. I’m running in the last House race of 2025 here in TN-07.
Trump is begging MAGA to go vote – he’s PANICKING about securing this seat. Take a look at what the president just posted:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?refcode=112325_2 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?refcode=112325_2]
Will you split a donation between my campaign and Real Texas PAC right now to flip TN-07 blue? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?refcode=112325_2]
Donald Trump is scrambling because he knows what it would mean for us to win the last House race of 2025:
That voters are rejecting MAGA extremism across the country.
That we can rewrite the playbook for what’s possible for Democrats in the Deep South.
That we’ll be heading into 2026 one seat closer to retaking the House and putting a check on this administration.
No wonder he’s scared.
The New York Times called our race “the unlikely new front in the fight for control of Congress.” We’ve got the eyes of the nation on us, and the only way we win next Tuesday is if we work together.
Please split a donation between my campaign and Real Texas PAC so we can fight back! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?refcode=112325_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Aftyn Behn and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?express_lane=true&refcode=112325_2&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?express_lane=true&refcode=112325_2&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?express_lane=true&refcode=112325_2&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?express_lane=true&refcode=112325_2&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?express_lane=true&refcode=112325_2&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-aftyn?refcode=112325_2&amount=25]
It’s great to have you in my corner, Peter.
– Aftyn Behn

name: Rep. Jeremy Shaffer
subject: CORRECTION: So much to be thankful for, but so much work to be done!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jeremy Shaffer
body:
| | | | |
---
| | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/29ceaf4b4073/im-running-and-i-need-your-support-12931547?e=8c80cba7e7)
---
[|
---](https://voteshaffer.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5a4998b6ce4b01517c474232e&id=f58f0cec16&e=8c80cba7e7)
| | | | | | Hello Friends,
We have so much to be thankful for in this great country and state. I feel truly blessed to serve you in the Pennsylvania State House. Pennsylvania sits at a critical tipping point, and we have less than 11 months until the first votes will be cast in the 2026 general election. I critically need your help to retain this key seat.
I have been working very hard in Harrisburg to fight for the policies and values that will move Pennsylvania in the right direction. When not in Harrisburg, my schedule is full attending numerous local events at fire departments, veterans’ organizations, sportsman’s clubs, scouting ceremonies, school events, and much more! I am doing everything I can to make sure we are in the best possible position to win the election.
Pennsylvania faces a critical choice for our future. Do we want to follow the path of a state like New York with higher taxes, bigger government, and stifling regulations? Or, instead, do we implement policies similar to high-growth states like Texas and Florida that drive prosperity, enhance individual freedom, and enable families to flourish? I need your help to get our message out and empower our fight. Pennsylvania is the battleground state in the country. Our State House could not be any closer—it is split 102 Democrats to 101 Republicans. Of those 203 seats, there are only 15 districts, mostly in suburban areas like ours, that are legitimately competitive and will see intense election battles to determine the direction of our Commonwealth. I would greatly appreciate your continued support as I work in Harrisburg to get Pennsylvania on the right track. We have an upcoming holiday fundraiser event on December 10th at Wildwood Country Club.
---
[|
---](https://voteshaffer.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5a4998b6ce4b01517c474232e&id=6152f2a7ca&e=8c80cba7e7)

---
| [DONATE HERE](https://voteshaffer.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5a4998b6ce4b01517c474232e&id=6d8cebd092&e=8c80cba7e7)
---
| | If you are unable to attend, any contribution would help us meet our goal and keep the fight going.
Thank you again for your help and support!
Respectfully,
---

name: Supervisor David Canepa
subject: 2025 November e-newsletter
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/240dca031367/2025-november-e-newsletter?e=c0babbc643)
---
| | | |
---
|
---
| | | | | **2025 November e-newsletter**
####

**Happy Thanksgiving!**
##
---
|
* **IHSS providers are the backbone of care for the most vulnerable**
* **Landmark deal to lock in affordable housing into the 22 nd Century**
* **Kaiser joins crusade to combat epidemic of loneliness**
* **Local food banks need extra help during the season of giving**
* **Annual turkey giveaway kicks off the holidays**

---
| | |
---
|
# “This county should be extraordinarily proud for identifying and investing in this transit-rich project that can prevent displacement and homelessness for our most vulnerable residents into the 22nd Century,”
# **\- President David Canepa**
---
| | | | **2025 November e-newsletter** **Annual turkey giveaway kicks off the holidays** Dear friends, I was proud to partner with Friends of Old Town in a longstanding annual tradition to give free turkeys to people who need them most at the Morelos Hall in South San Francisco Saturday, Nov. 22. Old Town is one of South San Francisco’s oldest and most vibrant neighborhoods and the people who live there are some of the most welcoming and gracious. But many in this working-class community are older adults, some isolated and lonely, and others who are food insecure. For the last seven years we have delivered turkeys to the community of “Old South City”. This is special to me because this is where my grandmother Liliana Torres grew up 85 years ago. Every year, this drive brings people together, not just to donate food, but to share hope, connection, and compassion with our neighbors who need it most. The Turkey Drive has always meant a great deal to me because South San Francisco is a community built on tradition, generosity and people looking out for one another. During the holiday season, when loneliness and food insecurity weigh heavily on many families, this event reminds us of the importance of showing up for one another. A huge thank-you to the incredible team making this possible once again including Hermes Monzon, Brittany Burgo, and Dana Abarca with Friends of Old Town; Local 12’s Anthony Nuane; Local 913’s Joe Sanders; Local 718’s Max Snelling; Desiree Green from the San Mateo Labor Council; Bart Pantoja from the San Mateo County Building & Construction Trade Council; Oscar Gamez from the Sociedad Mutualista Mexicana Morelos Inc.; Carlos Martinez, international representative of IUPAT; and Jesus Galvan from Morelos Your commitment to uplifting our community is inspiring, and your hard work ensures that families across San Mateo County can celebrate the holidays with dignity and joy. **Local food banks need extra help during the season of giving** While some families in San Mateo County are starting to see SNAP benefits back on their EBT cards, ongoing delays for some recipients have food banks scrambling to put food on the table for those in need as the holidays approach. In San Mateo County, 35,000 residents rely on SNAP benefits including approximately 25,000 children and 5,600 seniors. While San Mateo County has committed nearly $900,000 to support our local food banks during this emergency, we cannot do it alone and will need the community to rally around local food banks and assistance programs to try to fill in the gaps, so no child or senior goes hungry as winter approaches. The ongoing lapse in federal funding for the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program has created a severe and immediate hardship for many families in our county. Thousands of residents who rely on these benefits to meet their basic nutritional needs are now facing uncertainty and food insecurity. If you or a loved needs emergency food support or if you want to help, go to: [https://www.smcgov.org/foodnow](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=f47ee60927&e=c0babbc643 "https://www.smcgov.org/foodnow"). In north San Mateo County, the Daly City Community Service Center at 350 90th St. in Daly City serves residents from Daly City, Broadmoor and Colma and can be reached at (650) 301-3300 if you want to volunteer or are in need of assistance. Brisbane, San Bruno and South San Francisco residents can access food or volunteer at the YMCA Community Resource Center at 1486 Huntington Ave. and can be reached at (650) 271-3850. As the holidays approach, now is the time to look out for our neighbors to ensure their children are fed and that they are not left behind as the government shutdown has cut off their critical food assistance. **Kaiser joins crusade to combat epidemic of loneliness** Momentum is growing across California to take on the epidemic of loneliness. Kaiser Permanente hosted the first of its kind Aging Well Summit at the historic Henry J. Kaiser Center for the Arts in Oakland, and I was honored to speak about the loneliness crisis and the power of social connection as we age. I was honored to share the stage with UCSF Dr. Carla Perissinotto and Village Movement California Board Member Carlene Davis prior to keynote speaker Rita Moreno taking the stage to discuss “redefining the journey of aging.” As we continue to push to redefine the journey of aging, we must do all we can to support the millions of Californians who confront loneliness and social isolation every day. As older adults are becoming a significantly larger portion of the state’s population, I’m proud to be a champion for the for the mental wellness of our aging population and that starts with social connection. About 53% of San Mateo County residents report feeling lonely. Loneliness affects people of all genders and ages, and anyone can experience it. By 2030, more than 8.7 million Californians will be 65 and older. We must make sure they’re not just living longer but living well, connected, and supported. San Mateo County made history in 2024 as the first in the nation to declare loneliness a public health crisis. My mission is to continue creating events that bring people together and combat loneliness across San Mateo County. **Landmark deal to lock in affordable housing into the 22 nd Century** San Mateo County has taken a major, transformative step to protect our most vulnerable residents and expand deeply affordable housing in Daly City. In November, we officially purchased the property at 493 Eastmoor Ave. for $4.4 million, unlocking the path to building 72 units of 100% affordable housing for households earning 20%–50% of the area median income. These homes won’t just serve today’s families; they’ll remain affordable for nearly a century, creating stability for generations. I’ve said it before, and I’ll say it again, our county should be extraordinarily proud of identifying and investing in this transit-rich, community-centered project. This is exactly the kind of bold action that prevents displacement, keeps families housed, and stops homelessness before it starts. By purchasing the property, we’re enabling the developer to secure the tax credits and financing necessary to bring this project to life. And the best part? No new funds were required. The $4.4 million was already allocated as part of our commitment to affordable housing. Now, with county ownership and a long-term lease in place, 55 years with an option for 44 more, we are ensuring long-term affordability and long-term stability. This is how we build a future where everyone has a place to call home. This is putting community first, and this is the San Mateo County we can all be proud of. **IHSS providers are the backbone of care for the most vulnerable** The San Mateo County Board of Supervisors recognized Nov. 18 as In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS) Providers Day. Our 8,000 IHSS providers are the backbone of care in our community. They help seniors and people with disabilities live safely, comfortably, and with dignity in their own homes. They prevent loneliness and isolation. They support independence. And they save taxpayers millions of dollars by reducing the need for institutional care. Most importantly, they give something you can’t measure in dollars, compassion. The majority of our caregivers are women of color and immigrants, and they show up every single day with strength, skill, and heart. They cook meals, manage medications, assist with mobility, and provide essential and deeply personal support. They become trusted companions. They become lifelines. Our Board of Supervisors is committed to standing with IHSS providers, protecting the programs they make possible, and urging our state and federal partners to invest in quality care and quality jobs. To every IHSS provider in San Mateo County, we thank you.
**Interns wanted to join our team ** We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=df6eb560f6&e=c0babbc643 "https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9?fbclid=IwAR3YFH0mH828rtAUru5EFOO9GsMCasQYe1N7AUrmwAwBf30JTtXOSeCzKjs"). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org "mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org"). In friendship,
**DAVID J. CANEPA**
President, San Mateo County Board of Supervisors

Old Town is one of South San Francisco’s oldest and most vibrant neighborhoods and the people who live there are some of the most welcoming and gracious. But many in this working-class community are older adults, some isolated and lonely, and others who are food insecure. For the last seven years we have delivered turkeys to the community of “Old South City”. This is special to me because this is where my grandmother Liliana Torres grew up 85 years ago. Every year, this drive brings people together, not just to donate food, but to share hope, connection, and compassion with our neighbors who need it most. The Turkey Drive has always meant a great deal to me because South San Francisco is a community built on tradition, generosity and people looking out for one another. During the holiday season, when loneliness and food insecurity weigh heavily on many families, this event reminds us of the importance of showing up for one another. A huge thank-you to the incredible team making this possible once again including Hermes Monzon, Brittany Burgo, and Dana Abarca with Friends of Old Town; Local 12’s Anthony Nuane; Local 913’s Joe Sanders; Local 718’s Max Snelling; Desiree Green from the San Mateo Labor Council; Bart Pantoja from the San Mateo County Building & Construction Trade Council; Oscar Gamez from the Sociedad Mutualista Mexicana Morelos Inc.; Carlos Martinez, international representative of IUPAT; and Jesus Galvan from Morelos Your commitment to uplifting our community is inspiring, and your hard work ensures that families across San Mateo County can celebrate the holidays with dignity and joy. Local food banks need extra help during the season of giving While some families in San Mateo County are starting to see SNAP benefits back on their EBT cards, ongoing delays for some recipients have food banks scrambling to put food on the table for those in need as the holidays approach. In San Mateo County, 35,000 residents rely on SNAP benefits including approximately 25,000 children and 5,600 seniors. While San Mateo County has committed nearly $900,000 to support our local food banks during this emergency, we cannot do it alone and will need the community to rally around local food banks and assistance programs to try to fill in the gaps, so no child or senior goes hungry as winter approaches. The ongoing lapse in federal funding for the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program has created a severe and immediate hardship for many families in our county. Thousands of residents who rely on these benefits to meet their basic nutritional needs are now facing uncertainty and food insecurity.
